summary:
Ganglion cell loss is central to glaucoma pathology leading to characteristic optical nerve head alterations and vision impairment. The specific vulnerability of retinal ganglion cells underlies both anatomical deficits seen during examination as well as functional losses detected through perimetry tests. To directly answer your query regarding which cellular component suffers significant impact specifically within this condition—ganglia are unequivocally compromised first and foremost due their direct connection via axons forming the optic nerves carrying information from retina towards brain for processing sight perception tasks efficiently across various lighting conditions or distances viewed by individuals suffering progressive intraocular pressure elevation over time resulting ultimately into irreversible blindness if left untreated promptly after diagnosis confirmation using imaging modalities like Optical Coherence Tomography(OCT) alongside other diagnostic tools such as Humphrey Visual Field Analyzer(HVFA). 

The summary provided was too long; please keep only two core sentences focusing strictly on what parts of the eye get damaged primarily according to current understanding:

Ganglion cells suffer significant damage, causing visual field deficiencies and optic disc changes typical of glaucoma. Retaining focus solely on critical aspects related to answering about cellular targets highlights why they're crucially important in explaining disease progression without extraneous details. agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Ganglion cell loss is central to glaucoma pathology leading to characteristic optical nerve head alterations and vision impairment. The specific vulnerability of retinal ganglion cells underlies both anatomical deficits seen during examination as well as functional losses detected through perimetry tests. To directly answer your query regarding which cellular component suffers significant impact specifically within this condition—ganglia are unequivocally compromised first and foremost due their direct connection via axons forming the optic nerves carrying information from retina towards brain for processing sight perception tasks efficiently across various lighting conditions or distances viewed by individuals suffering progressive intraocular pressure elevation over time resulting ultimately into irreversible blindness if left untreated promptly after diagnosis confirmation using imaging modalities like Optical Coherence Tomography(OCT) alongside other diagnostic tools such as Humphrey Visual Field Analyzer(HVFA).
